name: libnet-smtp-ssl-perl
title: libnet-smtp-ssl-perl
summary: Perl module providing SSL support to Net::SMTP
description:
Perl module providing SSL support to Net::SMTP
Net::SMTP::SSL implements the same API as Net::SMTP, but uses IO::Socket::SSL
for its network operations. Due to the nature of Net::SMTP's new method, it is
not overridden to make use of a default port for the SMTPS service. Perhaps
future versions will be smart like that. Port 465 is usually what you want,
and it's not a pain to specify that.
.
This package is deprecated. Net::SMTP (in perl core) has support for SMTP
over SSL, and also for STARTTLS, since version 1.28 (included in Perl 5.22).
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libnet-smtp-ssl-perl because Perl module providing SSL support to Net::SMTP.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libnet-smtp-ssl-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libnet-smtp-ssl-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libnet-smtp-ssl-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
